# kanidm Provider

Terraform provider for managing Kanidm identity resources.

The provider manages Kanidm identities, groups, OAuth2 clients, application entries, and selected account/system policy objects.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    kanidm = {
      source = "seanlatimer/kanidm"
    }
  }
}

# Configure the Kanidm Provider with explicit credentials
provider "kanidm" {
  url   = "https://idm.s8i.ca"
  token = var.kanidm_token
}

# Alternative: Configure using environment variables
# export KANIDM_URL="https://idm.s8i.ca"
# export KANIDM_TOKEN="your-api-token"
provider "kanidm" {}

# Recommended: Use 1Password provider to retrieve credentials
terraform {
  required_providers {
    kanidm = {
      source = "seanlatimer/kanidm"
    }
    onepassword = {
      source  = "1Password/onepassword"
      version = "~> 2.1"
    }
  }
}

data "onepassword_item" "kanidm_admin_token" {
  vault = "Infrastructure"
  title = "Kanidm Admin Token"
}

provider "kanidm" {
  url   = "https://idm.s8i.ca"
  token = data.onepassword_item.kanidm_admin_token.credential
}
```

## Supported Resources

- `kanidm_person`
- `kanidm_service_account`
- `kanidm_application`
- `kanidm_group`
- `kanidm_group_members`
- `kanidm_account_policy`
- `kanidm_system_denied_names`
- `kanidm_oauth2_basic`
- `kanidm_oauth2_public`

## Notes

- Most resources use a stable UUID-backed `id` in state so external renames can be detected without losing resource identity.
- `kanidm_group_members` manages a subset of membership without taking ownership of the whole group member list.
- `kanidm_account_policy` is a separate resource so policies can be attached to both built-in and user-managed groups.
- `kanidm_system_denied_names` is a singleton-style resource and depends on healthy Kanidm system configuration entries.
- For lab or self-signed environments you can set `insecure_skip_verify = true` or `KANIDM_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY=true`.

## Guides

- [Getting Started](guides/getting-started.md)

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `insecure_skip_verify` (Boolean) Disable TLS certificate verification. Only use for testing against trusted self-signed endpoints. May also be enabled via KANIDM_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY.
- `person_defaults` (Attributes) Default management behavior for person resources. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--person_defaults))
- `token` (String, Sensitive) Kanidm API token for authentication. May also be provided via KANIDM_TOKEN environment variable.
- `url` (String) Kanidm server URL. May also be provided via KANIDM_URL environment variable.

<a id="nestedatt--person_defaults"></a>
### Nested Schema for `person_defaults`

Optional:

- `display_management` (String) Default management mode for person display names. Valid values: `managed`, `initial`.
- `generate_initial_credential_reset_token` (Boolean) Whether person resources should create an initial credential reset token during creation by default.
- `initial_credential_reset_token_ttl` (Number) Default TTL in seconds for initial credential reset tokens generated during person creation.
- `legalname_management` (String) Default management mode for person legal names. Valid values: `managed`, `initial`.
- `name_management` (String) Default management mode for person names. Valid values: `managed`, `initial`.
